caption: The video captures a series of presentation slides for a course titled "CRYOGENIC ENGINEERING" from NPTEL, presented by "Prof. M D Atrey, Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Bombay". The slides focus on "Tutorial – 2" and specifically the "Ideal Work Requirement". A thermodynamic graph is displayed, showing a process loop on a temperature-entropy (T-s) diagram with a line labeled "p=const". As the sequence progresses, an equation for work is introduced: "$\frac{W}{m} = T_1(s_1 - s_2) - (h_1 - h_2)$". In the final stage, a data table appears below the graph, providing values for pressure "p (bar)", temperature "T (K)", enthalpy "h (j/g)", and entropy "s (J/gK)" for states 1, 2, and f. The values listed include pressures of 1.013 and 202.6 bar, temperatures of 300 and 77 K, and various enthalpy and entropy readings.
